---

# function: validate_hostcidr

Accepts a string and returns `true` if it is a valid IPv4 CIDR notation AND the address is a usable host address: it must not be the network address (all host bits zero, e.g. `"10.10.10.0/24"`) nor the broadcast address (all host bits one, e.g. `"10.10.10.255/24"`).

## Example Usage

```terraform
output "valid_hostcidr" {
  value = provider::functions::validate_hostcidr("10.1.1.1/24") // true
}

output "invalid_hostcidr_net" {
  value = provider::functions::validate_hostcidr("10.1.1.0/24") // false
}

output "invalid_hostcidr_bcast" {
  value = provider::functions::validate_hostcidr("10.1.1.255/24") // false
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
validate_hostcidr(cidr string) bool
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `cidr` (String) The string to validate as an IPv4 host CIDR notation.
